title: GraphSpec
description: Storage schema for flow-based programming graphs. Boundary nodes (type='graphInput', 'graphOutput', 'graphProp') ARE the interface definition - no separate inputs/outputs/props arrays needed. This is the minimal canonical format for persistence.

NodeMeta.description: Position and optional metadata for visual layout.

PortRef.description: Reference to a port on a node within the same scope.

Edge.description: A connection between two ports within the same scope.

PropValue.description: A property value on a node instance.

ref.description: If true, value is a reference path to another property.

Node.description: A node instance in the graph. References a NodeDefinition by name. System types (graphInput/graphOutput/graphProp) are reserved names handled by the evaluator natively.

name.description: Unique instance name within the parent scope.

type.description: References a NodeDefinition by name. System types: graphInput, graphOutput, graphProp.

context.description: Optional context override for cross-context references. If omitted, inherits from the parent graph.

Graph.description: Top-level graph container. The context field determines which evaluator/runtime handles execution. Interface is defined by boundary nodes (kind='graphInput', 'graphOutput', 'graphProp').

context.description: Evaluation context/runtime (e.g., 'js', 'function', 'sql', 'workflow'). Determines which evaluator handles this graph.

PortDef.description: A port definition on a node type.

PropDef.description: A property definition on a node type.

NodeDefinition.description: Definition of a node type. Identity is the (context, name) tuple. When a graph field is present, this is a digital asset (composite node) whose implementation is the internal sub-graph. When absent, it is a leaf node requiring an external implementation.

context.description: Evaluation context/runtime this definition belongs to (e.g., 'js', 'function', 'sql', 'workflow'). Determines which evaluator can execute this node.

category.description: Category for editor palette organization (not part of identity).

props.description: Promoted parameters. For digital assets, these flow into graphProp boundary nodes in the internal graph.

graph.description: Internal sub-graph (digital asset). When present, boundary nodes (graphInput/graphOutput/graphProp) define the interface. The graph IS the implementation.

volatile.description: If true, this node must always be re-evaluated even when inputs haven't changed (e.g., http/fetch, time/now).

x-spec-rules: Boundary nodes ARE the interface: Nodes with kind='graphInput', 'graphOutput', or 'graphProp' define the graph's ports. The port/prop name is stored in the portName/propName property.
Edges are per-scope: Each node with children stores its own edges array.
Paths: Node identity is path-based (e.g., '/subnet1/nested/myNode'). Root is '/'.
Minimal storage: Only store what's needed to reconstruct the graph. Derived data is computed at runtime.
